solution: ```class Solution:
    def divisorGame(self, n: int) -> bool:
        return n % 2 == 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The key observation is that Alice wins if and only if n is even. When n is even, Alice can always subtract 1 (a valid divisor) to leave an odd number; any odd number has only odd divisors, so subtracting an odd divisor yields an even number again. Bob is eventually forced to face n = 2 and lose. When n is odd, Alice must subtract an odd divisor, leaving an even number for Bob, who can then mirror the strategy and force Alice to lose.
